definition: The internal organs of the body, especially those within the chest and abdomen; entrails.
context: You know? And, uh… finally, they are maddened by hunger and they just attack… [screams] And they got copilot blood all over them, copilot viscera falling out of their mouth on their pink shirts, you know?

definition: Persons who renounce or abandon their religious faith or political allegiance; those who stand apart from orthodox belief.
context: I should have been slaying apostates the entire time. Oh well, what are you going to do?

definition: Excessively complicated; characterized by deviousness or underhanded procedure; like the intricate bureaucracy of the Byzantine Empire.
context: I don't even have a plug. I got a whole Byzantine, you know, bunch of different plugs. Surge protectors, all sorts– because I don't want some janitor with a wide broom hitting my plug.

definition: To cause to disappear or dissolve; to become insubstantial or vanish from physical existence.
context: You know? And they don't even see anything. Because at that point you become just dematerialized. You know? You're nothing, you're stuff, basically.

definition: Persons or groups blamed for the wrongdoings or mistakes of others; originally a goat symbolically laden with sins and sent into the wilderness.
context: As a matter of fact, why not make the goats the scapegoats? Like in the old days. Yeah, sacrifice a goat. Make it his problem.

definition: A person excessively worried about having a serious illness; one who imagines symptoms of disease where none exist.
context: Here's the problem. You go to doctors too much, they start thinking you're a hypochondriac and then they don't take anything seriously, you know?

definition: In a manner relating to or characterized by an unhealthy mental state or attitude; excessively gloomy or concerned with disturbing subjects.
context: Now, we all went our different paths, but Jimmy became fat, then he became really fat, then he became obese, then he became morbidly obese. And now, 586 pounds.

definition: Characterized by subtle shades of meaning or expression; exhibiting fine distinctions in thought, feeling, or quality.
context: You know, every person knows that people are nuanced. They're not all evil or all good. You know? My dad did good stuff, you know?

definition: The making of amends for wrongdoing or injury; compensation paid for war damages or historical injustices.
context: Anyways, I look at it as a form of reparations. You know? I tell you, I've done my part, Goddamn it. Probably done your part too.

definition: Relating to or denoting a severe mental disorder in which thought and emotions are so impaired that contact with external reality is lost.
context: You know? Like, in your mind, in your picture, you have to have a picture there of you with a lady and you got to believe it so much that you don't even believe what's really happening. You know what I'm getting at? It's kind of like a psychotic break you got to do.

definition: Relating to schizophrenia, a long-term mental disorder involving a breakdown in the relation between thought, emotion, and behavior.
context: You're thinking of all these things in your head. You just got a psychotic break going that would rival a paranoid schizophrenic.

definition: The state of being subject to death; the quality or condition of being mortal; human death rate or destruction.
context: This guy was in a weird way reconciling his mortality. Hilariously in front of us. Ironically, he's no longer with us.

definition: Producing much fruit, foliage, or offspring; present in large numbers or quantities; plentiful; intellectually productive.
context: But we all love Norm and, my God, he is certainly prolific. Certainly energetic.

definition: Showing an ability to understand and share the feelings of another; characterized by empathy and emotional resonance.
context: And wildly, disarmingly empathetic. It's very rare that I'd meet somebody in a professional situation that made me put my dukes down like that.

definition: Resulting from or showing sincere and intense conviction; serious in intention, purpose, or effort; archaic: serious in a way that avoids frivolity.
context: I don't think my mother has ever spoken a word that had any irony in it. You know what I mean? She's just earnest, she's just happy.
